source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Chooses -2 because its magnitude is greater, ignoring that it is further from zero.

cognitive_load.justification: Quick recognition that the negative number closest to zero is larger.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Answers -1 because it is treated as the smaller absolute value.

cognitive_load.justification: Single comparison reinforcing order among negatives on the number line.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: Write these numbers from left to right on a number line: -10, -4, -17

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Places -4 first and -17 last by ordering by closeness to zero in the wrong direction.

cognitive_load.justification: Orders multiple negative integers according to their actual positions on the number line.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: The difference between P and Q is 24 on a negative number line. If Q is at -12 and P is to the left of Q, what is P?

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Answers 12 by moving right instead of left from -12.

cognitive_load.justification: Reteach item combining negative direction with interval size on a number line.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Answers False because the number with the greater magnitude is assumed to be greater.

cognitive_load.justification: Binary check of a core negative-number comparison fact.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Uses > by comparing only the positive parts 740 and 470.

cognitive_load.justification: Compares larger-magnitude negative integers while inserting the correct inequality symbol.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: Rewrite this statement so it is true: -3 < -5

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Keeps the incorrect direction or changes a number instead of correcting the inequality.

cognitive_load.justification: Requires correcting a false negative-number comparison rather than selecting from options.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: True or false: a = -8 satisfies the inequality a < -5

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Answers False because -8 is treated as larger than -5.

cognitive_load.justification: Reteach item that applies negative-number comparison within an inequality statement.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: Which list is in ascending order?

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Places the negative number closest to zero first in ascending order.

cognitive_load.justification: Recognition check for ordering a simple negative-only list.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: Write these numbers in ascending order: -3, 13, 3, -2, 0, 1

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Places -2 before -3 or inserts positive numbers before 0 incorrectly.

cognitive_load.justification: Mixed set of negative, zero and positive integers ordered from smallest to largest.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: Arrange these temperatures from lowest to highest: -8°C, 12°C, 9°C, -15°C, 11°C, -7°C, 2°C

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Places -7 before -15 because it is treated as the smaller temperature.

cognitive_load.justification: Transfers mixed-number ordering into a temperature context while keeping the underlying order structure clear.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: Write these numbers in ascending order: -0.4, -0.71, -0.38, -0.402, -0.25

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Orders the negatives as if they were positive decimals, for example -0.25 first and -0.71 last.

cognitive_load.justification: Reteach set that combines negative and decimal comparison, the highest-risk ordering case in this section.
